description: A Spanish rice dish traditionally associated with Valencia, cooked in a wide shallow pan and flavored with stock, saffron, and a range of ingredients such as seafood, chicken, rabbit, or vegetables.
core_ingredients: Aromatics such as onion or garlic
preparation_steps: Cook aromatics and any meat or seafood as required.
Add rice and seasonings, then coat briefly in the cooking fat.
Pour in stock and cook without excessive stirring so the rice cooks evenly.
Add ingredients in stages depending on cooking time.
Finish when the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed; rest briefly before serving.

nutrition.protein: Moderate to high depending on meat or seafood

health_advice.benefits: Can be a balanced one-pan meal with grains, protein, and vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Sodium can be high if salty stock, processed stock cubes, or cured meats are used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use plenty of vegetables, moderate oil, and lean protein choices.

dietary_notes.note: Rice-based paella is often naturally gluten-free, but packaged stock and seasonings should be checked.

allergens: Fish if fish stock is used
Possible sulfites in some seafood or stock products
